title_original: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 laying down harmonised rules on artificial intelligence (Artificial Intelligence Act)
issuing_body: European Parliament and Council of the European Union

key_dates.event: published in the Official Journal (OJ L, 2024/1689)
entered into force (Art 113)
prohibitions (Art 5) and AI-literacy obligations began to apply
GPAI-model obligations began to apply

Council final approval of the digital-omnibus amendment deferring high-risk obligations (Annex III to 2027-12-02; Annex I-embedded to 2028-08-02); amending act pending OJ publication as of last_verified — Art 50(1) transparency NOT deferred
Art 50 transparency obligations apply (unchanged by omnibus for 50(1); 50(2) watermarking deferred to 2026-12-02 for systems already on market)

summary_md: ■ FACT: Adopted 2024, in force since 2024-08-01, applying in stages; classified enacted_law per SPEC §9.3 (primary legislation despite the EU-law label 'Regulation'). ■ FACT: A June 2026 omnibus amendment — finally approved by the Council on 2026-06-29 and pending Official Journal publication as of 2026-07-12 — defers the Annex III high-risk obligations to 2027-12-02 and Annex I-embedded to 2028-08-02, while leaving Art 50(1) transparency applying from 2026-08-02. ▲ INFERENCE (medium): until the amending act is published, the dates on the books remain those of Reg 2024/1689; records here therefore carry the original applies_from with the deferral documented in key_dates.
analyst_notes: Pass 1 (authoring) 2026-07-12. Consilium press-release URL returned HTTP 403 to automated fetch (known bot-blocking host, MJ-7 allowlist); existence and content corroborated via multiple independent Tier-3 law-firm alerts and the Parliament endorsement of 2026-06-16. Re-verify OJ publication of the amending act before Pass 2 review; when published, update key_dates with the new CELEX and re-score any affected assessments (§19.5).

issuing_body: European Parliament and Council of the European Union

key_dates.event: published in the Official Journal (OJ L 119)
became fully applicable (Art 99(2))

summary_md: ■ FACT: Fully applicable since 2018-05-25; the EU's horizontal data-protection regime. Amendment history does not change its live status (amendment = event, not status — SPEC §8/MJ-4). ▲ INFERENCE (medium): Art 22's automated-decision-making right is the primary GDPR hook for agentic systems making consequential decisions (C8).
analyst_notes: Pass 1 (authoring) 2026-07-12. Instrument metadata verified against EUR-Lex CELEX record this session; Art 22 verbatim wording not re-fetched this session (paraphrase-only provision, confidence capped accordingly).

title_original: SB24-205 — Consumer Protections for Artificial Intelligence (Colorado)
title_en: Colorado AI Act (SB 24-205) — superseded before applicability

key_dates.event: signed by the Governor (became law)
original date for substantive developer/deployer duties (never reached — see analyst_notes for the SB25B-004 delay episode)
repealed and reenacted by SB26-189 (signed) — substantive duties never became applicable

summary_md: ■ FACT: Enacted 2024-05-17 as the first comprehensive US state AI law targeting algorithmic discrimination in consequential decisions; its substantive duties never took effect. ■ FACT: Repealed and reenacted by SB26-189 (signed 2026-05-14). ▲ INFERENCE (medium): the instrument is the taxonomy's exemplar of enacted_law → superseded WITHOUT ever reaching applicability — status (Axis C) and enactment (Axis A) are independent, exactly as SPEC §9 edge-case (a) requires.
analyst_notes: Pass 1 (authoring) 2026-07-12. Delay episode: SB25B-004 (special session, signed 2025-08-28 per corroborating Tier-3 reports) moved the duties date 2026-02-01 → 2026-06-30; not independently fetched this session, so recorded here rather than as a cited key_date. OPEN VERIFICATION ITEM for Pass 2: whether the SB26-189 repeal was operative on signing or on 2026-08-12 (standard non-safety-clause timing) — i.e. whether SB24-205 duties were technically live 2026-06-30→08-12. Status recorded as superseded per the practical consensus; confidence capped medium on that nuance.

key_dates.event: signed by the Governor; repeals and reenacts the 2024 Colorado AI Act
developer compliance obligations begin (technical documentation to deployers)

summary_md: ■ FACT: Signed 2026-05-14; repeals and reenacts the 2024 Colorado AI Act, replacing its duty-of-care/risk-management model with a disclosure- and transparency-centered framework for automated decision-making technology (ADMT) in consequential decisions (employment, housing, financial services, insurance, healthcare, education, government benefits). ■ FACT: Developer obligations apply from 2027-01-01. ▲ INFERENCE (medium): for agentic systems, the operative hook is consequential decision-making (C8) — the pivot from EU-style risk management to disclosure narrows the engineering surface to documentation and notice controls.
analyst_notes: Pass 1 (authoring) 2026-07-12. Verified via the official bill page fetch. Enforcement contingent on AG rulemaking per corroborating reports (not yet independently verified — Pass 2 item). Candidate near_term_hit driver for US assessments of C8-bearing agents once published (applies within 18 months of any assessed_date near today).

title_original: 人工智能生成合成内容标识办法
title_en: Measures for Labeling AI-Generated Synthetic Content
issuing_body: 国家互联网信息办公室 · 工业和信息化部 · 公安部 · 国家广播电视总局 (CAC, MIIT, MPS, NRTA)

key_dates.event: issued by four departments (国信办通字〔2025〕2号)
came into effect (施行)

summary_md: ■ FACT: Four-department departmental rules (classified regulation + binding per SPEC §8 rule 3) requiring explicit (显式) and implicit (隐式) labeling of AI-generated synthetic content; issued 2025-03-07, in effect since 2025-09-01. ▲ INFERENCE (medium): together with the deep-synthesis and generative-AI interim measures it forms the content-governance stack that drives prohibition_adjacent readings for public-content agents (C1/C6) in CN assessments — the Mira scenario's China story.
analyst_notes: Pass 1 (authoring) 2026-07-12. Verified by direct fetch of the CAC notice (Tier 1, Chinese authoritative text; verbatim definitions captured). Official Q&A (答记者问) states coordination with 深度合成规定 and 生成式人工智能服务管理暂行办法 — those two instruments to be added with their own Tier 1 verification in the next CN pass rather than cited second-hand here. English renderings in this record are the analyst's own translation — confidence capped per §7.5.

title_original: Model AI Governance Framework for Generative AI — Fostering a Trusted Ecosystem
title_en: Singapore Model AI Governance Framework for Generative AI (2024)

key_dates.event: published (PDF verified by direct fetch this session)

summary_md: ■ FACT: Voluntary governance framework for generative AI issued by the AI Verify Foundation and IMDA on 2024-05-30 (title, issuers, and date verified against the official PDF this session). ▲ INFERENCE (medium): as a voluntary_framework it is non_binding (§8 Axis B independent of Axis C: 'fully_applicable' here means published-and-operative as guidance); it typifies Singapore's assurance-first approach — market-expected adoption without legal mandate, so it never drives binding_hit but shapes enforcement_posture readings.
analyst_notes: Pass 1 (authoring) 2026-07-12. PDF text extraction was limited (compressed); the framework's nine governance dimensions and any agent-relevant sections are a Pass 2 full-text item — deliberately NOT asserted as fact here. PDPA and its 2020 amendment (MJ-4 amendment-as-event exemplar) queued for the next SG pass.

title_original: Executive Order 14179 of January 23, 2025 — Removing Barriers to American Leadership in Artificial Intelligence
title_en: EO 14179 — Removing Barriers to American Leadership in AI
issuing_body: The White House (President of the United States)

key_dates.event: signed
published in the Federal Register (90 FR 8741)

summary_md: ■ FACT (high): Signed 2025-01-23, published 90 FR 8741; sets the replacement federal AI policy ('sustain and enhance America's global AI dominance', Sec 2), directs an AI Action Plan (Sec 4), and orders agencies to suspend/revise/rescind actions taken under 'the revoked Executive Order 14110' (Sec 5(a)) and to revise OMB M-24-10/M-24-18 (Sec 5(b)). Full Tier 1 text read this session. ▲ INFERENCE (medium): conditionally_binding per SPEC §8 rule 4 — binds the executive branch, not private parties; for the Atlas its main effect is on enforcement_posture readings and the federal-procurement channel, not direct obligations on agent operators.
analyst_notes: Pass 1 (authoring) 2026-07-12. Full FR text read from the govinfo PDF. PRECISION NOTE vs SPEC Appendix A: EO 14179 does not itself revoke EO 14110 — Sec 5(a) refers to 'the revoked Executive Order 14110', i.e. revocation had already occurred (EO 14148 of 2025-01-20, §2 — to be independently verified Pass 2). Supersession links kept at the policy-replacement level. OMB M-24-10/M-24-18 revision status = Pass 2 item.

title_original: Executive Order 14110 of October 30, 2023 — Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy Development and Use of Artificial Intelligence
title_en: EO 14110 — Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy AI (rescinded)
issuing_body: The White House (President of the United States)

key_dates.event: signed

revoked by EO 14148 §2(ggg) — 'Executive Order 14110 of October 30, 2023 (Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy Development and Use of Artificial Intelligence)' listed among revocations (Tier 1 text read this session)

summary_md: ■ FACT (high): Revoked on 2025-01-20 by EO 14148 §2(ggg) (90 FR 8237; full Tier 1 text read this session), and referenced as 'the revoked Executive Order 14110' in EO 14179 Sec 5(a). ▲ INFERENCE (medium): retained in the corpus per SPEC §9 edge-case (b) — a rescinded executive action stays visible for historical mapping with supersession links; the 14110→14148→14179 chain is the taxonomy's executive-action supersession exemplar (revocation vehicle and replacement policy are DIFFERENT instruments — a precision most trackers collapse).
analyst_notes: Pass 1 (authoring) 2026-07-12, updated same day: revocation vehicle VERIFIED — EO 14148 §2(ggg), signed 2025-01-20, published 90 FR 8237. Former open item closed with Tier 1 evidence; status_date confidence now high. Original 14110 FR citation (88 FR 75191) still to be added from its own FR document in a later pass (not cited from memory).

title_original: 生成式人工智能服务管理暂行办法
title_en: Interim Measures for the Management of Generative AI Services
issuing_body: 七部门:国家互联网信息办公室、国家发展改革委、教育部、科技部、工业和信息化部、公安部、国家广播电视总局

key_dates.event: 公布 (国家互联网信息办公室令第15号, seven departments)
施行 (came into effect)

summary_md: ■ FACT (medium, translation cap): Seven-department interim measures for generative AI services (Order No. 15), published 2023-07-10, in effect since 2023-08-15; classified regulation + binding per SPEC §8 rule 3. ▲ INFERENCE (medium): Article 9 places network content-producer responsibility on the service PROVIDER — for agent operators this locates accountability at the operator, not the model vendor or the user, which is the pivotal allocation question for agentic deployments in CN.
analyst_notes: Pass 1 (authoring) 2026-07-12. Verified by direct CAC fetch (issuing departments, dates, 文号, Art 9 verbatim). English renderings are analyst translations (§7.5 cap). Algorithm/GenAI filing (备案) linkage and Art 17 labeling cross-reference to be detailed in a later pass.

title_original: 互联网信息服务深度合成管理规定
title_en: Provisions on the Administration of Deep Synthesis of Internet Information Services
issuing_body: 三部门:国家互联网信息办公室、工业和信息化部、公安部 (CAC, MIIT, MPS)

key_dates.event: 公布 (three departments)
施行 (came into effect)

summary_md: ■ FACT (medium, translation cap): Three-department deep-synthesis provisions, published 2022-12-11, in effect since 2023-01-10 — the earliest binding layer of the CN synthetic-content stack. ▲ INFERENCE (medium): Article 17's conspicuous-labeling duty names 智能对话 (intelligent dialogue) and 合成人声 (synthetic voice) among the functions requiring prominent marks — i.e., a conversational agent's interaction surface is EXPRESSLY within scope, not an interpretive stretch.
analyst_notes: Pass 1 (authoring) 2026-07-12. Verified by direct CAC fetch (departments, dates, Art 17 verbatim). Order number (令) not shown on the publication page — record and backfill in Pass 2. English renderings are analyst translations (§7.5 cap).
